title: Crumbly Ethiopian Soybean Crumble
description: A tangy, crumbly dish featuring soy sauce and other seasonings for a post-workout meal that's also fodmap-friendly.
instructions: Rinse and soak soybeans in water for at least 6 hours. Drain well.
In a large pot, heat oil over medium heat. Add soybeans and cook until they start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add chili powder, ginger paste, cumin seeds, black pepper, and salt. Stir well to combine with the beans for about 2-3 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat and let it thicken slightly (about 10-15 minutes).
Once thickened, use an immersion blender or transfer half of the mixture to a regular blender and puree until smooth. Return to pot.
Season with more salt if needed and serve warm.
